name: Arnold Ross
abstract: Arnold Ross (January 29, 1921, Boston - June 5, 2000, Culver City, California) was an American jazz pianist. Arnold learned clarinet, trumpet, and violin as a child before picking up piano when he was twelve years old.

about_quotes.speaker: Red Callender
about_quotes.evidence: So Arnold Ross, who I love dearly, refused to stay... He could have stayed anywhere, but he decided he was going to stay with us, because we were together.

about_quotes.speaker: Harry Edison
about_quotes.evidence: then I made an album here at the Hague, that nightclub across from the Ambassador Hotel, with Arnold Ross.

about_quotes.speaker: Gerald Wiggins
about_quotes.evidence: Spike's piano player Arnold Ross became ill, so Spike asked Helen could he borrow me for a while. So I went on the road with the Spike Jones band.
about_quotes.doc_title: Gerald Wiggins interviewed by Monk Rowe, Los Angeles, California, September 2, 1995

about_quotes.speaker: Bill Coleman
about_quotes.evidence: The other record was a 45 rpm on Imperial records and has Oscar still playing cello and features Arnold Ross, piano...

about_quotes.speaker: Patricia Willard
about_quotes.evidence: The interviewer lists Arnold Ross in a note; the interviewee does not mention him or claim any personal interaction.

about_quotes.speaker: Charlie Barnet
about_quotes.evidence: Arnold Ross was in that band and Paul Sarmento and Buddy Childress
